title: Delete dead code: internal/recovery, internal/streamtest, stale top-level dirs
description:
Context: verified-dead code with zero importers anywhere in the repo:
- internal/recovery/ (~1,100 LOC stale-tick recovery; grep for 'internal/recovery' finds no imports outside the package)
- internal/streamtest/ (a 6-line hello-world leftover)
- .ticker/ (stale checkpoints dir), .tickflow-bin/ (wrapper for the old Tickflow runner), prototypes/lit-shoelace-board/ (superseded by the embedded board), benchmarks/ (perf data artifacts)

Deliverable: remove all of the above in one commit.

Procedure: before deleting each item, re-verify zero references with grep across code, scripts, CI, and docs: grep -rn 'internal/recovery|internal/streamtest|tickflow-bin|\.ticker|prototypes/|benchmarks/' --include='*' . (excluding .git). If a doc or script references one (e.g. docs mentioning benchmarks/), update that reference in the same commit. Run go mod tidy afterward in case deleted packages were sole users of a dependency.

Test cases / verification:
- go build ./... succeeds
- go test ./... fully green
- the grep above returns no hits outside .git and the activity log

Files likely touched: deletions of the dirs listed above, possibly go.mod/go.sum (tidy), possibly a doc file referencing benchmarks.
acceptance_criteria: Listed dirs deleted with no dangling references (grep clean); go build ./... and go test ./... green; go.mod/go.sum tidied
